data.narrative:
Distributed Spectrum is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Alex Wulff, Ben Harpe, and Isaac Struhl that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies—including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $25M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, implying a $62M valuation. The Peer-Median method benchmarks against five same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables: Tenna Systems ($60M Seed), NODA AI ($125M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), and Stark Defence ($500M Series A). The median across this set is $150M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

These two methods triangulate to a range of $53M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $106M. The Stage-Progression method anchors the lower bound, while the Peer-Median approach supports the upper range. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in Series A peer valuations ($125M–$500M), which reflects differences in funding stage, commercial traction, and defense sector market conditions. Valuation sensitivity to product maturity and customer validation remains material at this stage.
